FOR REVIEWERS:
* [ ] .dockstore.yml: file is present and aligned with creator metadata in workflow. ORCID identifiers are strongly encouraged in creator metadata. The .dockstore.yml file is required to run tests
* [ ] In workflow: annotation field contains short description of what the workflow does. Should start with `This workflow does/runs/performs … xyz … to generate/analyze/etc …`
* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood

* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. FOR CONTRIBUTOR:
* [ ] I have read the [Adding workflows guidelines](https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/blob/main/workflows/README.md#adding-workflows)
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [ ] Please also take note of the reviewer guidelines below to facilitate a smooth review process.

FOR REVIEWERS:
* [ ] .dockstore.yml: file is present and aligned with creator metadata in workflow. ORCID identifiers are strongly encouraged in creator metadata. The .dockstore.yml file is required to run tests
* [ ] In workflow: annotation field contains short description of what the workflow does. Should start with `This workflow does/runs/performs … xyz … to generate/analyze/etc …`
* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood
* [ ] Workflow folder: prefer dash (`-`) over underscore (`_`), prefer all lowercase. Folder becomes repository in [iwc-workflows organization](https://github.com/iwc-workflows) and is included in TRS id
* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. If a similar workflow exists in IWC readme should explain differences with existing workflow and when one might prefer one workflow over another
* [ ] Changelog contains appropriate entries

FOR REVIEWERS:
* [ ] .dockstore.yml: file is present and aligned with creator metadata in workflow. ORCID identifiers are strongly encouraged in creator metadata. The .dockstore.yml file is required to run tests
* [ ] In workflow: annotation field contains short description of what the workflow does. Should start with `This workflow does/runs/performs … xyz … to generate/analyze/etc …`
* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood
* [ ] Workflow folder: prefer dash (`-`) over underscore (`_`), prefer all lowercase. Folder becomes repository in [iwc-workflows organization](https://github.com/iwc-workflows) and is included in TRS id
* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. If a similar workflow exists in IWC readme should explain differences with existing workflow and when one might prefer one workflow over another

FOR REVIEWERS:
* [ ] .dockstore.yml: file is present and aligned with creator metadata in workflow. ORCID identifiers are strongly encouraged in creator metadata. The .dockstore.yml file is required to run tests
* [ ] In workflow: annotation field contains short description of what the workflow does. Should start with `This workflow does/runs/performs … xyz … to generate/analyze/etc …`
* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood
* [ ] Workflow folder: prefer dash (`-`) over underscore (`_`), prefer all lowercase. Folder becomes repository in [iwc-workflows organization](https://github.com/iwc-workflows) and is included in TRS id
* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. If a similar workflow exists in IWC readme should explain differences with existing workflow and when one might prefer one workflow over another
* [ ] Changelog contains appropriate entries

* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. * [ ] I have read the [Adding workflows guidelines](https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/blob/main/workflows/README.md#adding-workflows)
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [ ] Please also take note of the reviewer guidelines below to facilitate a smooth review process.

FOR REVIEWERS:
* [ ] .dockstore.yml: file is present and aligned with creator metadata in workflow. ORCID identifiers are strongly encouraged in creator metadata. The .dockstore.yml file is required to run tests
* [ ] In workflow: annotation field contains short description of what the workflow does. Should start with `This workflow does/runs/performs … xyz … to generate/analyze/etc …`
* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood
* [ ] Workflow folder: prefer dash (`-`) over underscore (`_`), prefer all lowercase. Folder becomes repository in [iwc-workflows organization](https://github.com/iwc-workflows) and is included in TRS id
* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. If a similar workflow exists in IWC readme should explain differences with existing workflow and when one might prefer one workflow over another
* [ ] Changelog contains appropriate entries

FOR CONTRIBUTOR:
* [ ] I have read the [Adding workflows guidelines](https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/blob/main/workflows/README.md#adding-workflows)
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [ ] Please also take note of the reviewer guidelines below to facilitate a smooth review process.

FOR REVIEWERS:
* [ ] .dockstore.yml: file is present and aligned with creator metadata in workflow. ORCID identifiers are strongly encouraged in creator metadata. The .dockstore.yml file is required to run tests
* [ ] In workflow: annotation field contains short description of what the workflow does. Should start with `This workflow does/runs/performs … xyz … to generate/analyze/etc …`
* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood
* [ ] Workflow folder: prefer dash (`-`) over underscore (`_`), prefer all lowercase. Folder becomes repository in [iwc-workflows organization](https://github.com/iwc-workflows) and is included in TRS id
* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. If a similar workflow exists in IWC readme should explain differences with existing workflow and when one might prefer one workflow over another
* [ ] Changelog contains appropriate entries
* [ ] Large files (> 100 KB) are uploaded to zenodo and location urls are used in test - [x] Workflow imported from [usegalaxy.eu](https://usegalaxy.eu/) using `planemo workflow_test_init` to use a valid invocation

- [x] .dockstore.yml written

Thanks :smiley:

FOR CONTRIBUTOR:
* [ ] I have read the [Adding workflows guidelines](https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/blob/main/workflows/README.md#adding-workflows)
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [ ] Please also take note of the reviewer guidelines below to facilitate a smooth review process.

FOR REVIEWERS:
* [ ] .dockstore.yml: file is present and aligned with creator metadata in workflow. ORCID identifiers are strongly encouraged in creator metadata. The .dockstore.yml file is required to run tests
* [ ] In workflow: annotation field contains short description of what the workflow does. Should start with `This workflow does/runs/performs … xyz … to generate/analyze/etc …`
* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood
* [ ] Workflow folder: prefer dash (`-`) over underscore (`_`), prefer all lowercase. Folder becomes repository in [iwc-workflows organization](https://github.com/iwc-workflows) and is included in TRS id
* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. If a similar workflow exists in IWC readme should explain differences with existing workflow and when one might prefer one workflow over another
* [ ] Changelog contains appropriate entries

FOR CONTRIBUTOR:
* [ ] I have read the [Adding workflows guidelines](https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/blob/main/workflows/README.md#adding-workflows)
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [ ] Please also take note of the reviewer guidelines below to facilitate a smooth review process.

FOR REVIEWERS:
* [ ] .dockstore.yml: file is present and aligned with creator metadata in workflow. ORCID identifiers are strongly encouraged in creator metadata. The .dockstore.yml file is required to run tests
* [ ] In workflow: annotation field contains short description of what the workflow does. Should start with `This workflow does/runs/performs … xyz … to generate/analyze/etc …`
* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood
* [ ] Workflow folder: prefer dash (`-`) over underscore (`_`), prefer all lowercase. Folder becomes repository in [iwc-workflows organization](https://github.com/iwc-workflows) and is included in TRS id
* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. If a similar workflow exists in IWC readme should explain differences with existing workflow and when one might prefer one workflow over another
* [ ] Changelog contains appropriate entries

+# metaQuantome 2: Function
+metaQuantome software suite Easterly et al. 2019 was developed by the Team for quantitative and statistical analysis of metaproteomics data. For taxonomic and functional expression analysis within the microbial community, metaQuantome leverages peptide-level quantitative information to generate visual outputs for data interpretation. It also generates outputs that help in understanding the taxonomic contribution to a selected function as well as functions expressed by selected taxonomic FOR CONTRIBUTOR:
* [ ] I have read the [Adding workflows guidelines](https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/blob/main/workflows/README.md#adding-workflows)
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [ ] Please also take note of the reviewer guidelines below to facilitate a smooth review process.

FOR REVIEWERS:
* [ ] .dockstore.yml: file is present and aligned with creator metadata in workflow. ORCID identifiers are strongly encouraged in creator metadata. The .dockstore.yml file is required to run tests
* [ ] In workflow: annotation field contains short description of what the workflow does. Should start with `This workflow does/runs/performs … xyz … to generate/analyze/etc …`
* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood
* [ ] Workflow folder: prefer dash (`-`) over underscore (`_`), prefer all lowercase. Folder becomes repository in [iwc-workflows organization](https://github.com/iwc-workflows) and is included in TRS id
* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. If a similar workflow exists in IWC readme should explain differences with existing workflow and when one might prefer one workflow over another
* [ ] Changelog contains appropriate entries

FOR CONTRIBUTOR:
* [ ] I have read the [Adding workflows guidelines](https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/blob/main/workflows/README.md#adding-workflows)
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [ ] Please also take note of the reviewer guidelines below to facilitate a smooth review process.

FOR REVIEWERS:
* [ ] .dockstore.yml: file is present and aligned with creator metadata in workflow. ORCID identifiers are strongly encouraged in creator metadata. The .dockstore.yml file is required to run tests
* [ ] In workflow: annotation field contains short description of what the workflow does. Should start with `This workflow does/runs/performs … xyz … to generate/analyze/etc …`
* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood
* [ ] Workflow folder: prefer dash (`-`) over underscore (`_`), prefer all lowercase. Folder becomes repository in [iwc-workflows organization](https://github.com/iwc-workflows) and is included in TRS id
* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. If a similar workflow exists in IWC readme should explain differences with existing workflow and when one might prefer one workflow over another
* [ ] Changelog contains appropriate entries

* [ ] I have read the [Adding workflows guidelines](https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/blob/main/workflows/README.md#adding-workflows)

FOR REVIEWERS:
* [ ] .dockstore.yml: file is present and aligned with creator metadata in workflow. ORCID identifiers are strongly encouraged in creator metadata. The .dockstore.yml file is required to run tests
* [ ] In workflow: annotation field contains short description of what the workflow does. Should start with `This workflow does/runs/performs … xyz … to generate/analyze/etc …`
* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood
* [ ] Workflow folder: prefer dash (`-`) over underscore (`_`), prefer all lowercase. Folder becomes repository in [iwc-workflows organization](https://github.com/iwc-workflows) and is included in TRS id
* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. If a similar workflow exists in IWC readme should explain differences with existing workflow and when one might prefer one workflow over another

* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood

* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. * [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood

* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. * [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood
* [ ] Workflow folder: prefer dash (`-`) over underscore (`_`), prefer all lowercase. Folder becomes repository in [iwc-workflows organization](https://github.com/iwc-workflows) and is included in TRS id
* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. If a similar workflow exists in IWC readme should explain differences with existing workflow and when one might prefer one workflow over another
* [ ] Changelog contains appropriate entries

CONTRIBUTOR:
* [ ] I have read the [Adding workflows guidelines](https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/blob/main/workflows/README.md#adding-workflows)
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [ ] Please also take note of the reviewer guidelines below to facilitate a smooth review process.

FOR REVIEWERS:
* [ ] .dockstore.yml: file is present and aligned with creator metadata in workflow. ORCID identifiers are strongly encouraged in creator metadata. The .dockstore.yml file is required to run tests
* [ ] In workflow: annotation field contains short description of what the workflow does. Should start with `This workflow does/runs/performs … xyz … to generate/analyze/etc …`
* [ ] In workflow: workflow inputs and outputs have human readable names (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless it is generally understood. Altering input or output labels requires adjusting these labels in the the workflow-tests.yml file as well
* [ ] In workflow: `name` field should be human readable (spaces are fine, no underscore, dash only where spelling dictates it), no abbreviation unless generally understood
* [ ] Workflow folder: prefer dash (`-`) over underscore (`_`), prefer all lowercase. Folder becomes repository in [iwc-workflows organization](https://github.com/iwc-workflows) and is included in TRS id
* [ ] Readme explains what workflow does, what are valid inputs and what outputs users can expect. If a tutorial or other resources exist they can be linked. If a similar workflow exists in IWC readme should explain differences with existing workflow and when one might prefer one workflow over another
* [ ] Changelog contains appropriate entries
* [ ] Large files (> 100 KB) are uploaded to zenodo and location urls are used in test file

----

In the README I would not discuss inputs/outputs per tool but at the WF level to avoid redundancy.
You can just list the inputs, their expected format and what the WF will use them for.

----

request"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-12-10T14:41:00Z","updated_at":"2024-12-16T15:17:07Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"We have a team-wide hackathon on 16+17 of December, hopefully we can hack on a few of these items:

### For website:

- [ ] Improve header component, welcome page
- [ ] Improve Galaxy URL selector for iwc.galaxyproject.org
  - [ ]  (also fix https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/issues/618)
- [ ] Include planemo run instructions
- [ ] Generate and include planemo job template
- [ ] Include most recent version in preview component, last update time
- [ ] Scrape dockstore categories and group workflows by category

### For planemo:
- [ ] extend linting
  - [ ] license check: https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/pull/617/files#r1862374391
  - [ ] improve changelog linting: https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/pull/617/files#r1862380758
  - [ ] workflow linting that flag email post job action: https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/pull/617/files#r1862360462
- [ ] improve console output for workflow run
  - [ ] Print steps completed, steps left, currently running steps
    - [ ] (explore https://github.com/Textualize/rich)
- [ ] Generate README.md and CHANGELOG.md entries (https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/issues/1482)
- [ ] Replace .dockstore.yml with .iwc.yml and generate artifacts from that file
  - [ ] allow marking workflows / tests as needing external server, on demand stuff like omero
 
### For workflows:
- [ ] Fix existing linting issues (missing readme, changelog)
- [ ] Fix workflow names, workflow parameter label to match pull request guidelines (Human readable)

### Galaxy
- [ ] [Sample sheets] (https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19305)
- [ ] Take Pause step out of experimental state

- [ ] Include planemo run instructions
- [ ] Generate and include planemo job template
- [ ] Include most recent version in preview component, last update time
- [ ] Scrape dockstore categories and group workflows by category

- [ ] extend linting
  - [ ] license check: https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/pull/617/files#r1862374391
  - [ ] improve changelog linting: https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/pull/617/files#r1862380758
  - [ ] workflow linting that flag email post job action: https://github.com/galaxyproject/iwc/pull/617/files#r1862360462
- [ ] improve console output for workflow run
  - [ ] Print steps completed, steps left, currently running steps
    - [ ] (explore https://github.com/Textualize/rich)
- [ ] Generate README.md and CHANGELOG.md entries (https://github.com/galaxyproject/planemo/issues/1482)
- [ ] Replace .dockstore.yml with .iwc.yml and generate artifacts from that file
  - [ ] allow marking workflows / tests as needing external server, on demand stuff like omero
 
### For workflows:

IRMA","user":{"login":"aaronKol","id":183315928,"node_id":"U_kgDOCu0t2A","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/183315928?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/aaronKol","html_url":"https://github.com/aaronKol","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aaronKol/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aaronKol/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aaronKol/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aaronKol/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aaronKol/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aaronKol/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aaronKol/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aaronKol/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/aaronKol/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Adding a wrapper for the tool IRMA (Iterative Refinement Meta-Assembler).

Problem: The IRMA variable `SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC` doesnt set the thread usage. IRMA will start a subprocess for every (8) virus segment. The variable `SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC` determines on how many threads each of these subprocess should run. E.g. `SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC=4` would yield 4 times 8 = 32 subprocesses. 
I did not find a way to restrict cores used by irma.  

FOR CONTRIBUTOR:
* [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document and this tool is appropriate for the tools-iuc repo.
* [x] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [x] This PR adds a new tool or tool collection

a wrapper for the tool IRMA (Iterative Refinement Meta-Assembler).

Problem: The IRMA variable `SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC` doesnt set the thread usage. IRMA will start a subprocess for every (8) virus segment. The variable `SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC` determines on how many threads each of these subprocess should run. E.g. `SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC=4` would yield 4 times 8 = 32 subprocesses. 
I did not find a way to restrict cores used by irma.  

FOR CONTRIBUTOR:

a wrapper for the tool IRMA (Iterative Refinement Meta-Assembler).

Problem: The IRMA variable `SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC` doesnt set the thread usage. IRMA will start a subprocess for every (8) virus segment. The variable `SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC` determines on how many threads each of these subprocess should run. E.g. `SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC=4` would yield 4 times 8 = 32 subprocesses. 
I did not find a way to restrict cores used by irma.  

FOR CONTRIBUTOR:
* [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document and this tool is appropriate for the tools-iuc repo.
* [x] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [x] This PR adds a new tool or tool collection
* [ ] This PR updates an existing tool or tool collection

Problem: The IRMA variable `SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC` doesnt set the thread usage. IRMA will start a subprocess for every (8) virus segment. The variable `SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC` determines on how many threads each of these subprocess should run. E.g. `SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC=4` would yield 4 times 8 = 32 subprocesses. 
I did not find a way to restrict cores used by irma.

My gut is though I would implement this as changing:
> 
> ```
>                 <option value="txt">Using lines in a simple text file.</option>
>                 <option value="tabular">Map original identifiers to new ones using a two column table.</option>
> ```
> 
> to:
> 
> ```
>                 <option value="txt">Using lines in a simple text file.</option>
>                 <option value="tabular">Map original identifiers to new ones using the first two columns of a simple table.</option>
>                 <option value="tabular_configured">Map original identifiers to new ones using arbitrary columns from a table.</option>
> ```
> 

So if I go with this part of @jmchilton's suggestion (i.e. hide the new behavior behind a new third select option, that would qualify for a WORKFLOW_SAFE_TOOL_VERSION_UPDATE and *not* require a new file then, correct?
Because all old workflows could then also run with the new tool version? @jmchilton I considered your fancier configuration ideas, but did not implement them here because:
- specifying header lines to ignore is covered by non-strict mode (although explicit header handling would still be a bit stricter than that)
- different column separators could be interesting for some use cases, but most other separators are also valid chars in element identifiers, making support for them a questionable general concept. toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/iuc/snpeff/snpEff_build_gb/5.2+galaxy0

Recent changes to turning off the db check seems to have problems. The warning box in blue goes away then pops back up and prevents the tool from submitting. Any server, several different test and real data runs. Has anyone been able to get this to work after the late Oct updates?

Workaround for end users:
1. Create your snpeffdb with SnpEff build 4.3+T.galaxy6 first
2. Then use SnpEff eff 4.3+T.galaxy2
3. Note that you might need to submit the job a few times to get it to go through

Reported at https://help.galaxyproject.org/t/snpeff-database-compatibility/13923/5


One of the test histories: https://usegalaxy.eu/u/jenj/h/test-snpeff-build

Screenshots: notice how the message will repeat and populate again if you click on the submit button a few times with v 5.2. Doing the repeat submission will allow a 4.3 job to eventually submit and run. 

![Screen Shot 2024-11-18 at 10 28 39 AM](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4d46cf7d-925b-49fc-b65f-b763f4a26bec)


![Screen Shot 2024-11-18 at 10 39 21 AM](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c05e8c4a-7f3f-42dd-90c5-a8d30f33f814)

toolshed.g2.bx.psu.edu/repos/iuc/snpeff/snpEff_build_gb/5.2+galaxy0

Recent changes to turning off the db check seems to have problems. The warning box in blue goes away then pops back up and prevents the tool from submitting. Any server, several different test and real data runs. Has anyone been able to get this to work after the late Oct updates?

Workaround for end users:
1. Create your snpeffdb with SnpEff build 4.3+T.galaxy6 first
2. Then use SnpEff eff 4.3+T.galaxy2
3. Note that you might need to submit the job a few times to get it to go through

Reported at https://help.galaxyproject.org/t/snpeff-database-compatibility/13923/5


One of the test histories: https://usegalaxy.eu/u/jenj/h/test-snpeff-build

Screenshots: notice how the message will repeat and populate again if you click on the submit button a few times with v 5.2. Doing the repeat submission will allow a 4.3 job to eventually submit and run.

the bug**

The current version of the tool creates two output collections with identical names and collection types in the history, but correct content in the history.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Run *Harmonize two collections* on a simple list as its first and a list of (fastq) pairs as its second input
2. Observe that name and type of the second output collection are identical to those of the first one even though the type should be list of pairs
3. Try to run bwa-mem on the second output collection and it will fail to find .forward/.reverse attributes of inputs

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/6388","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/6388/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/6388/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/6388/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/issues/6388","id":2558841843,"node_id":"I_kwDOAW4Y0s6YhNPz","number":6388,"title":"IUC tool or not?","user":{"login":"nomadscientist","id":44605769,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ0NjA1NzY5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/44605769?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadscientist","html_url":"https://github.com/nomadscientist","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadscientist/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadscientist/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadscientist/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadscientist/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadscientist/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadscientist/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadscientist/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadscientist/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nomadscientist/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-10-01T10:37:58Z","updated_at":"2024-10-01T12:12:57Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I want to predominantly use IUC tools, because they are sustained best by the community.
Determining whether a tool is an IUC tool or not is not trivial, however - there can be false positives if following a link to a toolshed, and also we should not have to follow a link to a toolshed.

Workflows have a nice IUC label on them, can tools?

My favorite way of learning more about a tool would currently be "View tool source", which lists the toolshed address at the very top, which in turn contains the repo owner.

otoh, this is also simplifying things too much: there are definitely better and worse wrappers even among the iuc ones, and there can be very good wrappers that are not iuc ones.
imo, the training material, iwc WFs, and to some extent the Help forum are all better ways to learn about recommended tools than just relying on who wrote a wrapper, and eventually, a visit of the toolshed repo is necessary anyway for a full picture (like when has the wrapper been updated last time, where is the code getting developed if not in tools-iuc, etc.).

A good place for the owner and possibly other toolshed info could be next to the requirements, references and co section (currently at the bottom of the tool interface). Bug fixes:
- ensures custom databases always have a valid filename as their path

Enhancements:
- more standardized naming of custom db records by asking for a name and for source info
- for all build modes: adjust default minimizer-spaces setting to match the upstream change in https://github.com/DerrickWood/kraken2/commit/f7268d1dacb6e5f1a2e5a775f49f5150b9e7cad2
- add bio.tools xref and update citation

CONTRIBUTOR:
* [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document and this tool is appropriate for the tools-iuc repo.
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [ ] This PR adds a new tool or tool collection
* [x] This PR updates an existing tool or tool collection
* [ ] This PR does something else (explain below)

Bug fixes:
- ensures custom databases always have a valid filename as their path

Enhancements:
- more standardized naming of custom db records by asking for a name and for source info
- for all build modes: adjust default minimizer-spaces setting to match the upstream change in https://github.com/DerrickWood/kraken2/commit/f7268d1dacb6e5f1a2e5a775f49f5150b9e7cad2
- add bio.tools xref and update citation

Requirement update:
- use Python 3.13

CONTRIBUTOR:
* [x] I have read the [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document and this tool is appropriate for the tools-iuc repo.
* [ ] License permits unrestricted use (educational + commercial)
* [ ] This PR adds a new tool or tool collection
* [x] This PR updates an existing tool or tool collection
* [ ] This PR does something else (explain below)

Bug fixes:
- ensures custom databases always have a valid filename as their path

Enhancements:
- more standardized naming of custom db records by asking for a name and for source info
- for all build modes: adjust default minimizer-spaces setting to match the upstream change in https://github.com/DerrickWood/kraken2/commit/f7268d1dacb6e5f1a2e5a775f49f5150b9e7cad2
- add bio.tools xref and update citation

Requirement update:
- use Python 3.13

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/pulls/comments/1820555578","pull_request_review_id":2401470434,"id":1820555578,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAW4Y0s5sg3k6","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,303 @@
+<tool id="IRMA" name="IRMA" version="@TOOL_VERSION@+galaxy@VERSION_SUFFIX@" profile="23.1">
+    <description>Construct robust assembly of highly variable RNA viruses</description>
+    <macros>
+        <token name="@TOOL_VERSION@">1.2.0</token>
+        <token name="@VERSION_SUFFIX@">0</token>
+    </macros>
+    <xrefs>
+        <xref type="bio.tools">irma-virus</xref> 
+    </xrefs>
+    <requirements>
+        <requirement type="package" version="@TOOL_VERSION@">IRMA</requirement>
+    </requirements>
+    <command detect_errors="exit_code"><![CDATA[
+        IRMA 
+        "${module}"
+        #set $input_type = $input_type_conditional.input_type
+        #if $input_type == "single"
+        "${input_type_conditional.fastq_input1}"
+        #elif $input_type == "paired_collection"
+        "${input_type_conditional.fastq_input.forward}" "${input_type_conditional.fastq_input.reverse}"
+        #end if
+        resultDir
+
+        &&
+        
+        python3 $__tool_directory__/createMissingFiles.py
+
+    ]]></command>
+    <configfiles>
+        <configfile filename="irma_config.sh"><![CDATA[#if $advanced_config.customize == "yes":
+
+### general
+SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC=$advanced_config.general.SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC
+DOUBLE_LOCAL_PROC=$advanced_config.general.DOUBLE_LOCAL_PROC	
+TMP=$advanced_config.general.TMP
+
+### reference
+SKIP_E=$advanced_config.reference.SKIP_E
+
+### read gathering
+MAX_ROUNDS=$advanced_config.read_gathering.MAX_ROUNDS		                        
+USE_MEDIAN=$advanced_config.read_gathering.USE_MEDIAN		                        
+QUAL_THRESHOLD=$advanced_config.read_gathering.QUAL_THRESHOLD	                    
+MIN_LEN=$advanced_config.read_gathering.MIN_LEN         		                    
+INCL_CHIM=$advanced_config.read_gathering.INCL_CHIM     		                    
+ADAPTER=$advanced_config.read_gathering.ADAPTER                              
+ENFORCE_CLIPPED_LENGTH=1$advanced_config.read_gathering.ENFORCE_CLIPPED_LENGTH	    
+MERGE_SECONDARY=$advanced_config.read_gathering.MERGE_SECONDARY         		    
+RESIDUAL_ASSEMBLY_FACTOR=$advanced_config.read_gathering.RESIDUAL_ASSEMBLY_FACTOR   
+
+### match step
+MIN_RP=$advanced_config.match_step.MIN_RP    
+MIN_RC=$advanced_config.match_step.MIN_RC
+MIN_BLAT_MATCH=$advanced_config.match_step.MIN_BLAT_MATCH
+
+### sort step
+SORT_PROG=$advanced_config.sort_step.SORT_PROG
+NONSEGMENTED=$advanced_config.sort_step.NONSEGMENTED
+
+## ALIGN STEP ##
+ALIGN_PROG=$advanced_config.align_step.ALIGN_PROG
+DEL_TYPE=$advanced_config.align_step.DEL_TYPE
+
+### FINISHING ASSEMBLY ###
+MAX_ITER_ASSEM=$advanced_config.assembly.MAX_ITER_ASSEM
+NO_MERGE=$advanced_config.assembly.NO_MERGE            
+INS_T=$advanced_config.assembly.INS_T
+DEL_T=$advanced_config.assembly.DEL_T
+INS_T_DEPTH=$advanced_config.assembly.INS_T_DEPTH
+DEL_T_DEPTH=$advanced_config.assembly.DEL_T_DEPTH
+SILENCE_COMPLEX_INDELS=$advanced_config.assembly.SILENCE_COMPLEX_INDELS
+MIN_AMBIG=$advanced_config.assembly.MIN_AMBIG
+SSW_M=$advanced_config.assembly.SSW_M
+SSW_X=$advanced_config.assembly.SSW_X
+SSW_O=$advanced_config.assembly.SSW_O
+SSW_E=$advanced_config.assembly.SSW_E
+ALIGN_AMENDED=$advanced_config.assembly.ALIGN_AMENDED
+
+### VARIANT CALLING ###
+AUTO_F=$advanced_config.variant_calling.AUTO_F
+MIN_FI=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_FI
+MIN_FD=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_FD
+MIN_F=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_F
+MIN_C=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_C
+MIN_AQ=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_AQ
+MIN_TCC=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_TCC
+MIN_CONF=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_CONF
+SIG_LEVEL=$advanced_config.variant_calling.SIG_LEVEL
+
+            #end if
+        ]]></configfile>                        
+    </configfiles>
+    <inputs>
+        <conditional name="input_type_conditional">
+            <param name="input_type" type="select" label="Input Type">
+                <option value="single" selected="true">Single Dataset</option>
+                <option value="paired_collection">Paired Collection</option>
+            </param>
+            <when value="single">
+                 <param name="fastq_input1" type="data" format="fastqsanger" label="Select fastq dataset" help="Specify dataset with single reads"/>
+            </when>
+            <when value="paired_collection">
+                 <param name="fastq_input" type="data_collection" format="fastqsanger" collection_type="paired" label="Select dataset pair" help="Specify paired dataset collection containing paired reads"/>
+            </when>
+        </conditional>
+
+        <param name="module" type="select" label="Select Module">
+            <option value="FLU" selected="true">FLU</option>
+            <option value="FLU-utr">FLU-utr</option>
+            <option value="FLU-pacbio">FLU-pacbio</option>
+            <option value="EBOLA">EBOLA</option>
+        </param>
+

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/pulls/comments/1820558410","pull_request_review_id":2401474944,"id":1820558410,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAW4Y0s5sg4RK","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,303 @@
+<tool id="IRMA" name="IRMA" version="@TOOL_VERSION@+galaxy@VERSION_SUFFIX@" profile="23.1">
+    <description>Construct robust assembly of highly variable RNA viruses</description>
+    <macros>
+        <token name="@TOOL_VERSION@">1.2.0</token>
+        <token name="@VERSION_SUFFIX@">0</token>
+    </macros>
+    <xrefs>
+        <xref type="bio.tools">irma-virus</xref> 
+    </xrefs>
+    <requirements>
+        <requirement type="package" version="@TOOL_VERSION@">IRMA</requirement>
+    </requirements>
+    <command detect_errors="exit_code"><![CDATA[
+        IRMA 
+        "${module}"
+        #set $input_type = $input_type_conditional.input_type
+        #if $input_type == "single"
+        "${input_type_conditional.fastq_input1}"
+        #elif $input_type == "paired_collection"
+        "${input_type_conditional.fastq_input.forward}" "${input_type_conditional.fastq_input.reverse}"
+        #end if
+        resultDir
+
+        &&
+        
+        python3 $__tool_directory__/createMissingFiles.py
+
+    ]]></command>
+    <configfiles>
+        <configfile filename="irma_config.sh"><![CDATA[#if $advanced_config.customize == "yes":
+
+### general
+SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC=$advanced_config.general.SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC
+DOUBLE_LOCAL_PROC=$advanced_config.general.DOUBLE_LOCAL_PROC	
+TMP=$advanced_config.general.TMP
+
+### reference
+SKIP_E=$advanced_config.reference.SKIP_E
+
+### read gathering
+MAX_ROUNDS=$advanced_config.read_gathering.MAX_ROUNDS		                        
+USE_MEDIAN=$advanced_config.read_gathering.USE_MEDIAN		                        
+QUAL_THRESHOLD=$advanced_config.read_gathering.QUAL_THRESHOLD	                    
+MIN_LEN=$advanced_config.read_gathering.MIN_LEN         		                    
+INCL_CHIM=$advanced_config.read_gathering.INCL_CHIM     		                    
+ADAPTER=$advanced_config.read_gathering.ADAPTER                              
+ENFORCE_CLIPPED_LENGTH=1$advanced_config.read_gathering.ENFORCE_CLIPPED_LENGTH	    
+MERGE_SECONDARY=$advanced_config.read_gathering.MERGE_SECONDARY         		    
+RESIDUAL_ASSEMBLY_FACTOR=$advanced_config.read_gathering.RESIDUAL_ASSEMBLY_FACTOR   
+
+### match step
+MIN_RP=$advanced_config.match_step.MIN_RP    
+MIN_RC=$advanced_config.match_step.MIN_RC
+MIN_BLAT_MATCH=$advanced_config.match_step.MIN_BLAT_MATCH
+
+### sort step
+SORT_PROG=$advanced_config.sort_step.SORT_PROG
+NONSEGMENTED=$advanced_config.sort_step.NONSEGMENTED
+
+## ALIGN STEP ##
+ALIGN_PROG=$advanced_config.align_step.ALIGN_PROG
+DEL_TYPE=$advanced_config.align_step.DEL_TYPE
+
+### FINISHING ASSEMBLY ###
+MAX_ITER_ASSEM=$advanced_config.assembly.MAX_ITER_ASSEM
+NO_MERGE=$advanced_config.assembly.NO_MERGE            
+INS_T=$advanced_config.assembly.INS_T
+DEL_T=$advanced_config.assembly.DEL_T
+INS_T_DEPTH=$advanced_config.assembly.INS_T_DEPTH
+DEL_T_DEPTH=$advanced_config.assembly.DEL_T_DEPTH
+SILENCE_COMPLEX_INDELS=$advanced_config.assembly.SILENCE_COMPLEX_INDELS
+MIN_AMBIG=$advanced_config.assembly.MIN_AMBIG
+SSW_M=$advanced_config.assembly.SSW_M
+SSW_X=$advanced_config.assembly.SSW_X
+SSW_O=$advanced_config.assembly.SSW_O
+SSW_E=$advanced_config.assembly.SSW_E
+ALIGN_AMENDED=$advanced_config.assembly.ALIGN_AMENDED
+
+### VARIANT CALLING ###
+AUTO_F=$advanced_config.variant_calling.AUTO_F
+MIN_FI=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_FI
+MIN_FD=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_FD
+MIN_F=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_F
+MIN_C=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_C
+MIN_AQ=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_AQ
+MIN_TCC=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_TCC
+MIN_CONF=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_CONF
+SIG_LEVEL=$advanced_config.variant_calling.SIG_LEVEL
+
+            #end if
+        ]]></configfile>                        
+    </configfiles>
+    <inputs>
+        <conditional name="input_type_conditional">
+            <param name="input_type" type="select" label="Input Type">
+                <option value="single" selected="true">Single Dataset</option>
+                <option value="paired_collection">Paired Collection</option>
+            </param>
+            <when value="single">
+                 <param name="fastq_input1" type="data" format="fastqsanger" label="Select fastq dataset" help="Specify dataset with single reads"/>
+            </when>
+            <when value="paired_collection">
+                 <param name="fastq_input" type="data_collection" format="fastqsanger" collection_type="paired" label="Select dataset pair" help="Specify paired dataset collection containing paired reads"/>
+            </when>
+        </conditional>
+
+        <param name="module" type="select" label="Select Module">
+            <option value="FLU" selected="true">FLU</option>
+            <option value="FLU-utr">FLU-utr</option>
+            <option value="FLU-pacbio">FLU-pacbio</option>
+            <option value="EBOLA">EBOLA</option>
+        </param>
+
+        <param name="optional_outputs" type="select" display="checkboxes" multiple="true" optional="true" label="Define IRMA outputs">
+            <option value="bam" selected="true">Bam: Alignment files of fastq against polished reference</option>
+            <option value="vcf" selected="true">Vcf: Variaton calling file per segment</option>
+        </param>
+        
+        <conditional name="advanced_config">
+            <param name="customize" type="select" label="Customize Advanced Settings?" help="Choosing Yes here, lets you overwrite advanced settings defined in IRMA's default configuration file.">

+import glob
+import os
+import subprocess
+
+dirPrefix = "resultDir/"
+expectedSegments = ["A_MP", "A_NP", "A_HA", "A_PB1",
+                    "A_PB2", "A_NA", "A_PA", "A_NS"]
+
+
+def renameSubtypeFiles(identifier):
+    files = glob.glob(dirPrefix + "A_" + identifier + "_*.*")
+    for file in files:
+        ext = file.split('.')[-1]
+        os.rename(file, dirPrefix + "A_" + identifier + "." + ext)
+
+
+def getMissingSegments():
+    presentSegments = [filename.split('.')[0].split('/')[-1]
+                       for filename in glob.glob(dirPrefix + "*.fasta")]
+    return [segment for segment in expectedSegments
+            if segment not in presentSegments]
+
+
+def getBamHeaderFromAnyFile():
+    anyBamFile = glob.glob(dirPrefix + "*.bam")[0]
+    samtoolsCmd = "samtools view -H " + anyBamFile
+    result = subprocess.run(samtoolsCmd, shell=True,
+                            stdout=subprocess.PIPE, text=True)
+    header = result.stdout.split('\n')[0]
+    return header
+
+
+def writeEmptyBam(identifier, bamHeader):
+    with open("headerSamFile.sam", "w") as f:
+        f.write(bamHeader)  # write header to a temporary sam file
+    cmd = "samtools view -H -b headerSamFile.sam > " \
+          + dirPrefix + identifier + ".bam"  # convert to bam
+    if subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True).returncode == 0:
+        os.remove("headerSamFile.sam")  # delete temporary sam file
+    else:
+        raise ValueError("Could not create bam file!")
+
+

+    open(dirPrefix + identifier + "." + ext, +import glob
+import os
+import subprocess
+
+dirPrefix = "resultDir/"
+expectedSegments = ["A_MP", "A_NP", "A_HA", "A_PB1",
+                    "A_PB2", "A_NA", "A_PA", "A_NS"]
+
+
+def renameSubtypeFiles(identifier):
+    files = glob.glob(dirPrefix + "A_" + identifier + "_*.*")
+    for file in files:
+        ext = file.split('.')[-1]
+        os.rename(file, dirPrefix + "A_" + identifier + "." + ext)
+
+
+def getMissingSegments():
+    presentSegments = [filename.split('.')[0].split('/')[-1]
+                       for filename in glob.glob(dirPrefix + "*.fasta")]
+    return [segment for segment in expectedSegments
+            if segment not in presentSegments]
+
+
+def getBamHeaderFromAnyFile():
+    anyBamFile = glob.glob(dirPrefix + "*.bam")[0]
+    samtoolsCmd = "samtools view -H " + anyBamFile
+    result = subprocess.run(samtoolsCmd, shell=True,
+                            stdout=subprocess.PIPE, text=True)
+    header = result.stdout.split('\n')[0]
+    return header
+
+
+def writeEmptyBam(identifier, bamHeader):
+    with open("headerSamFile.sam", "w") as f:
+        f.write(bamHeader)  # write header to a temporary sam file
+    cmd = "samtools view -H -b headerSamFile.sam > " \
+          + dirPrefix + identifier + ".bam"  # convert to bam
+    if subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True).returncode == 0:
+        os.remove("headerSamFile.sam")  # delete temporary sam file
+    else:
+        raise ValueError("Could not create bam file!")
+
+
+def writeEmptyFile(identifier, ext):
+    open(dirPrefix + identifier + "." + ext, 'a').close()
+
+
+def samtoolsSortAllBam():
+    for segment in expectedSegments:
+        os.rename(dirPrefix + segment + ".bam",
+                  dirPrefix + segment + "_unsorted.bam")
+        cmd = "samtools sort " + dirPrefix + segment + "_unsorted.bam > " \
+              + dirPrefix + segment + ".bam"
+        if subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True).returncode == 0:
+            os.remove(dirPrefix + segment + "_unsorted.bam")
+        else:
+            raise ValueError("Could not sort bam +import glob
+import os
+import subprocess
+
+dirPrefix = "resultDir/"
+expectedSegments = ["A_MP", "A_NP", "A_HA", "A_PB1",
+                    "A_PB2", "A_NA", "A_PA", "A_NS"]
+
+
+def renameSubtypeFiles(identifier):
+    files = glob.glob(dirPrefix + "A_" + identifier + "_*.*")
+    for file in files:
+        ext = file.split('.')[-1]
+        os.rename(file, dirPrefix + "A_" + identifier + "." + ext)
+
+
+def getMissingSegments():
+    presentSegments = [filename.split('.')[0].split('/')[-1]
+                       for filename in glob.glob(dirPrefix + "*.fasta")]
+    return [segment for segment in expectedSegments
+            if segment not in presentSegments]
+
+
+def getBamHeaderFromAnyFile():
+    anyBamFile = glob.glob(dirPrefix + "*.bam")[0]
+    samtoolsCmd = "samtools view -H " + anyBamFile
+    result = subprocess.run(samtoolsCmd, shell=True,
+                            stdout=subprocess.PIPE, text=True)
+    header = result.stdout.split('\n')[0]
+    return header
+
+
+def writeEmptyBam(identifier, bamHeader):
+    with open("headerSamFile.sam", "w") as f:
+        f.write(bamHeader)  # write header to a temporary sam file
+    cmd = "samtools view -H -b headerSamFile.sam > " \
+          + dirPrefix + identifier + ".bam"  # convert to bam
+    if subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True).returncode == 0:
+        os.remove("headerSamFile.sam")  # delete temporary sam file
+    else:
+        raise ValueError("Could not create bam file!")
+
+
+def writeEmptyFile(identifier, ext):
+    open(dirPrefix + identifier + "." + ext, 'a').close()
+
+
+def samtoolsSortAllBam():
+    for segment in expectedSegments:
+        os.rename(dirPrefix + segment + ".bam",
+                  dirPrefix + segment + "_unsorted.bam")
+        cmd = "samtools sort " + dirPrefix + segment + "_unsorted.bam > " \
+              + dirPrefix + segment + ".bam"
+        if subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True).returncode == 0:
+            os.remove(dirPrefix + segment + "_unsorted.bam")
+        else:
+            raise ValueError("Could not sort bam file!")
+
+

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/pulls/comments/1820619630","pull_request_review_id":2401571004,"id":1820619630,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAW4Y0s5shHNu","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,67 @@
+import glob
+import os
+import subprocess
+
+dirPrefix = "resultDir/"
+expectedSegments = ["A_MP", "A_NP", "A_HA", "A_PB1",
+                    "A_PB2", "A_NA", "A_PA", "A_NS"]
+
+
+def renameSubtypeFiles(identifier):
+    files = glob.glob(dirPrefix + "A_" + identifier + "_*.*")
+    for file in files:
+        ext = file.split('.')[-1]
+        os.rename(file, dirPrefix + "A_" + identifier + "." + ext)
+
+
+def getMissingSegments():
+    presentSegments = [filename.split('.')[0].split('/')[-1]
+                       for filename in glob.glob(dirPrefix + "*.fasta")]
+    return [segment for segment in expectedSegments
+            if segment not in presentSegments]
+
+
+def getBamHeaderFromAnyFile():
+    anyBamFile = glob.glob(dirPrefix + "*.bam")[0]
+    samtoolsCmd = "samtools view -H " + anyBamFile
+    result = subprocess.run(samtoolsCmd, shell=True,
+                            stdout=subprocess.PIPE, text=True)
+    header = result.stdout.split('\n')[0]","path":"tools/irma/createMissingFiles.py","commit_id":"212e31d789b93f526e6f8a2ed3ec56a1a516afba","original_commit_id":"212e31d789b93f526e6f8a2ed3ec56a1a516afba","user":{"login":"wm75","id":4742081,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ3NDIwODE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4742081?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75","html_url":"https://github.com/wm75","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"Why are you keeping only the first line? {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/pulls/comments/1820631826","pull_request_review_id":2401590735,"id":1820631826,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAW4Y0s5shKMS","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,67 @@
+import glob
+import os
+import subprocess
+
+dirPrefix = "resultDir/"
+expectedSegments = ["A_MP", "A_NP", "A_HA", "A_PB1",
+                    "A_PB2", "A_NA", "A_PA", "A_NS"]
+
+
+def renameSubtypeFiles(identifier):
+    files = glob.glob(dirPrefix + "A_" + identifier + "_*.*")
+    for file in files:
+        ext = file.split('.')[-1]
+        os.rename(file, dirPrefix + "A_" + identifier + "." + ext)
+
+
+def getMissingSegments():
+    presentSegments = [filename.split('.')[0].split('/')[-1]
+                       for filename in glob.glob(dirPrefix + "*.fasta")]
+    return [segment for segment in expectedSegments
+            if segment not in presentSegments]
+
+
+def getBamHeaderFromAnyFile():
+    anyBamFile = glob.glob(dirPrefix + "*.bam")[0]
+    samtoolsCmd = "samtools view -H " + anyBamFile
+    result = subprocess.run(samtoolsCmd, shell=True,
+                            stdout=subprocess.PIPE, text=True)
+    header = result.stdout.split('\n')[0]
+    return header
+
+
+def writeEmptyBam(identifier, bamHeader):
+    with open("headerSamFile.sam", "w") as f:
+        f.write(bamHeader)  # write header to a temporary sam file
+    cmd = "samtools view -H -b headerSamFile.sam > " \
+          + dirPrefix + identifier + ".bam"  # convert to bam
+    if subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True).returncode == 0:
+        os.remove("headerSamFile.sam")  # delete temporary sam file
+    else:
+        raise ValueError("Could not create bam file!")
+
+
+def writeEmptyFile(identifier, ext):
+    open(dirPrefix + identifier + "." + ext, 'a').close()
+
+
+def samtoolsSortAllBam():
+    for segment in expectedSegments:
+        os.rename(dirPrefix + segment + ".bam",
+                  dirPrefix + segment + "_unsorted.bam")
+        cmd = "samtools sort " + dirPrefix + segment + "_unsorted.bam > " \
+              + dirPrefix + segment + ".bam"
+        if subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True).returncode == 0:
+            os.remove(dirPrefix + segment + "_unsorted.bam")
+        else:
+            raise ValueError("Could not sort bam file!")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    renameSubtypeFiles("HA")
+    renameSubtypeFiles("NA")
+    for segment in getMissingSegments():
+        writeEmptyBam(segment, getBamHeaderFromAnyFile())
+        writeEmptyFile(segment, "fasta")
+        writeEmptyFile(segment, "vcf")","path":"tools/irma/createMissingFiles.py","commit_id":"212e31d789b93f526e6f8a2ed3ec56a1a516afba","original_commit_id":"212e31d789b93f526e6f8a2ed3ec56a1a516afba","user":{"login":"wm75","id":4742081,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ3NDIwODE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/4742081?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75","html_url":"https://github.com/wm75","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wm75/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"for {"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/tools-iuc/pulls/comments/1820638556","pull_request_review_id":2401601187,"id":1820638556,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAW4Y0s5shL1c","diff_hunk":"@@ -0,0 +1,418 @@
+<tool id="irma" name="IRMA" version="@TOOL_VERSION@+galaxy@VERSION_SUFFIX@" profile="23.1">
+    <description>Construct robust assembly of highly variable RNA viruses</description>
+    <macros>
+        <token name="@TOOL_VERSION@">1.2.0</token>
+        <token name="@VERSION_SUFFIX@">0</token>
+    </macros>
+    <xrefs>
+        <xref type="bio.tools">irma-virus</xref> 
+    </xrefs>
+    <requirements>
+        <requirement type="package" version="@TOOL_VERSION@">irma</requirement>
+    </requirements>
+    <command detect_errors="exit_code"><![CDATA[
+        mkdir ./irma_temp &&
+        export IRMA_GALAXY_TEMP=\$PWD/irma_temp &&
+
+        IRMA 
+        '${module}'
+        #set $input_type = $input_type_conditional.input_type
+        #if $input_type == 'single'
+        '${input_type_conditional.fastq_input1}'
+        #elif $input_type == 'paired_collection'
+        '${input_type_conditional.fastq_input.forward}'
+        '${input_type_conditional.fastq_input.reverse}'
+        #end if
+        resultDir
+
+        &&
+        
+        python3 '$__tool_directory__/createMissingFiles.py'
+
+    ]]></command>
+    <configfiles>
+        <configfile filename='irma_config.sh'><![CDATA[
+
+SINGLE_LOCAL_PROC=\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-4}
+DOUBLE_LOCAL_PROC=\$((\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-4} / 2))
+TMP=\$IRMA_GALAXY_TEMP
+
+        #if $advanced_config.customize == 'yes':
+
+### reference
+SKIP_E=$advanced_config.SKIP_E
+
+### read gathering
+MAX_ROUNDS=$advanced_config.read_gathering.MAX_ROUNDS		                        
+USE_MEDIAN=$advanced_config.read_gathering.USE_MEDIAN		                        
+QUAL_THRESHOLD=$advanced_config.read_gathering.QUAL_THRESHOLD	                    
+MIN_LEN=$advanced_config.read_gathering.MIN_LEN         		                    
+INCL_CHIM=$advanced_config.read_gathering.INCL_CHIM     		                    
+ADAPTER=$advanced_config.read_gathering.ADAPTER                              
+ENFORCE_CLIPPED_LENGTH=1$advanced_config.read_gathering.ENFORCE_CLIPPED_LENGTH	    
+MERGE_SECONDARY=$advanced_config.read_gathering.MERGE_SECONDARY         		    
+RESIDUAL_ASSEMBLY_FACTOR=$advanced_config.read_gathering.RESIDUAL_ASSEMBLY_FACTOR   
+
+### match step
+MIN_RP=$advanced_config.match_step.MIN_RP    
+MIN_RC=$advanced_config.match_step.MIN_RC
+MIN_BLAT_MATCH=$advanced_config.match_step.MIN_BLAT_MATCH
+
+### sort step
+SORT_PROG=$advanced_config.sort_step.SORT_PROG
+NONSEGMENTED=$advanced_config.sort_step.NONSEGMENTED
+
+## ALIGN STEP ##
+ALIGN_PROG=$advanced_config.align_step.ALIGN_PROG
+DEL_TYPE=$advanced_config.align_step.DEL_TYPE
+
+### FINISHING ASSEMBLY ###
+MAX_ITER_ASSEM=$advanced_config.assembly.MAX_ITER_ASSEM
+NO_MERGE=$advanced_config.assembly.NO_MERGE            
+INS_T=$advanced_config.assembly.INS_T
+DEL_T=$advanced_config.assembly.DEL_T
+INS_T_DEPTH=$advanced_config.assembly.INS_T_DEPTH
+DEL_T_DEPTH=$advanced_config.assembly.DEL_T_DEPTH
+SILENCE_COMPLEX_INDELS=$advanced_config.assembly.SILENCE_COMPLEX_INDELS
+MIN_AMBIG=$advanced_config.assembly.MIN_AMBIG
+SSW_M=$advanced_config.assembly.SSW_M
+SSW_X=$advanced_config.assembly.SSW_X
+SSW_O=$advanced_config.assembly.SSW_O
+SSW_E=$advanced_config.assembly.SSW_E
+ALIGN_AMENDED=$advanced_config.assembly.ALIGN_AMENDED
+
+### VARIANT CALLING ###
+AUTO_F=$advanced_config.variant_calling.AUTO_F
+MIN_FI=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_FI
+MIN_FD=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_FD
+MIN_F=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_F
+MIN_C=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_C
+MIN_AQ=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_AQ
+MIN_TCC=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_TCC
+MIN_CONF=$advanced_config.variant_calling.MIN_CONF
+SIG_LEVEL=$advanced_config.variant_calling.SIG_LEVEL
+
+            #end if
+        ]]></configfile>                        
+    </configfiles>
+    <inputs>
+        <conditional name="input_type_conditional">
+            <param name="input_type" type="select" label="Input Type">
+                <option value="single" selected="true">Single Dataset</option>
+                <option value="paired_collection">Paired Collection</option>
+            </param>
+            <when value="single">
+                 <param name="fastq_input1" type="data" format="fastqsanger" label="Select fastq dataset" help="Specify dataset with single reads"/>
+            </when>
+            <when value="paired_collection">
+                 <param name="fastq_input" type="data_collection" format="fastqsanger" collection_type="paired" label="Select dataset pair" help="Specify paired dataset collection containing paired reads"/>
+            </when>
+        </conditional>
+
+        <param name="module" type="select" label="Select Module">
+            <option value="FLU" selected="true">FLU</option>
+            <option value="FLU-utr">FLU-utr</option>
+            <option value="FLU-pacbio">FLU-pacbio</option>
+        </param>
+
+        <param name="optional_outputs" type="select" display="checkboxes" multiple="true" optional="true" label="Define IRMA outputs">
+            <option value="bam" selected="true">Bam: Alignment files of fastq against polished reference</option>
+            <option value="vcf" selected="true">Vcf: Variaton calling file per segment</option>
+        </param>
+        
+        <conditional name="advanced_config">
+            <param name="customize" type="select" label="Customize Advanced Settings?" help="Choosing Yes here, lets you overwrite advanced settings defined in IRMA's default configuration file.">
+                <option value="no">No, use defaults for advanced settings</option>
+                <option value="yes">Yes</option>
+            </param>
+            <when value="no" />
+            <when value="yes">
+                <param name="SKIP_E" type="select" label="Skip reference elongation?">
+                    <option value="0">No</option>
+                    <option value="1" selected="true">Yes</option>
+                </param>
+                <section name="read_gathering" title="Settings to read gathering" expanded="false">
+                    <param name="MAX_ROUNDS" type="integer" min="1" max="20" value="5" label="Maximal rounds of read gathering"/>
+                    <param name="USE_MEDIAN" type="select" label="Use median or average for read gathering">
+                        <option value="0">Average</option>
+                        <option value="1" selected="true">Median</option>
+                    </param>
+                    <param name="QUAL_THRESHOLD" type="integer" min="0" value="30" label="Minimum read statistic to keep (Phred)"/>
+                    <param name="MIN_LEN" type="integer" min="0" max="1000" value="125" label="Minimum read length to keep (bp)" />
+                    <param name="INCL_CHIM" type="select" label="Include chimera? Transposase adapter - clips 5' of the adapter on the forward strand and 3' on the reverse strand. applicable to NextTera pair-end reads">
+                        <option value="1">Yes</option>
+                        <option value="0" selected="true">No</option>
+                    </param>
+                    <param name="ADAPTER" type="text" value="AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG" label="Adapter sequence">
+                        <validator type="regex">[CAGT]+</validator> 
+                    </param>
+                    <param name="ENFORCE_CLIPPED_LENGTH" type="select" label="Reads are filtered for minimum length post adapter trimming">
+                        <option value="1" selected="true">Yes</option>
+                        <option value="0">No</option>
+                    </param>
+                    <param name="MERGE_SECONDARY" type="select" label="Merge secondary data after the first round to the unmatched pool. Useful if co-infections are not expected.">
+                        <option value="1">Yes</option>
+                        <option value="0" selected="true">No</option>
+                    </param>
+                    <param name="RESIDUAL_ASSEMBLY_FACTOR" type="integer" min="0" max="100" value="0" label="The ratio of match read patterns to altmatch is measured, and if its less than factor, residual assembly is performed. Set 0 for off."/>
+                </section>
+                <section name="match_step" title="Settings regarding the match step" expanded="false" >
+                    <param name="MIN_RP" type="integer" min="0" value="15" label="Minimum read pattern count to continue"/>
+                    <param name="MIN_RC" type="integer" min="0" value="15" label="Minimum read count to continue"/>
+                    <param name="MIN_BLAT_MATCH" type="integer" min="0" value="0" label="Minimum read pattern count to continue"/>
+                </section>
+                <section name="sort_step" title="Settings regarding the sort step" expanded="false">
+                    <param name="SORT_PROG" type="select" label="Select program used for sorting">
+                        <option value="LABEL">LABEL</option>
+                        <option value="BLAT" selected="true">BLAT</option>
+                    </param>
+                    <param name="NONSEGMENTED" type="select" label="Activate: Segmented">
+                        <option value="1">No</option>
+                        <option value="0" selected="true">Yes</option>
+                    </param>
+                </section>
+                <section name="align_step" title="Settings regarding the alignment step" expanded="false">
+                    <param name="ALIGN_PROG" type="select" label="Select program used for alignment">
+                        <option value="BLAT">BLAT</option>
+                        <option value="SAM" selected="true">SAM</option>
+                    </param>
+                    <param name="DEL_TYPE" type="select" label="How to handle deletions in rough alignment">
+                        <option value="" selected="true">Default</option>
+                        <option value="NNN">NNN</option>
+                        <option value="REF">REF</option>
+                        <option value="DEL" >DEL</option>
+                    </param>
+                </section>
+                <section name="assembly" title="Settings regarding the assembly" expanded="false">
+                    <param name="MAX_ITER_ASSEM" type="integer" min="1" value="5" label="Maximal assembly iteration"/>
+                    <param name="NO_MERGE" type="select" label="Merge read pairs">
+                        <option value="1">Yes</option>
+                        <option value="0" selected="true">No</option>
+                    </param>
+                    <param name="INS_T" type="float" min="0" max="1" value="0.25" label="The minimum frequenncy threshold for insertion refinement"/>
+                    <param name="DEL_T" type="float" min="0" max="1" value="0.6" label="The minimum frequenncy threshold for deletion refinement"/>
+                    <param name="INS_T_DEPTH" type="integer" min="1" value="1" label="The minimum coverage depth for insertion refinement"/>
+                    <param name="DEL_T_DEPTH" type="integer" min="1" value="1" label="The minimum coverage depth for deletion refinement"/>
+                    <param name="SILENCE_COMPLEX_INDELS" type="select" label="Silences reads with complex indels (having 4 indels with at least one greater than 9)">
+                        <option value="1">Yes</option>
+                        <option value="0" selected="true">No</option>
+                    </param>
+                    <param name="MIN_AMBIG" type="float" min="0" max="1" value="0.25" label="The minimum called SNV frequency for mixed base in amended consensus folder"/>
+                    <param name="SSW_M" type="integer" min="1" value="2" label="The smith-waterman match score"/>
+                    <param name="SSW_X" type="integer" min="1" value="5" label="The smith-waterman mismatch penalty"/>
+                    <param name="SSW_O" type="integer" min="1" value="10" label="The smith-waterman gap open penalty"/>
+                    <param name="SSW_E" type="integer" min="1" value="1" label="The smith-waterman gap extension penalty"/>
+                    <param name="ALIGN_AMENDED" type="select" label="Do global alignment of the amended consensus to the HMM profile">
+                        <option value="1">Yes</option>
+                        <option value="0" selected="true">No</option>
+                    </param>
+                </section>
